utility_evaluation.REASONING:
The required format has 6 items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. The final property solution includes all six sections. Now check if each is fully specified with all details:
- property: includes selected property with details and price (address/type/size/price/condition/features) — fully specified.
- financing: includes financing solution with terms (mortgage type, loan amount, down payment, interest rate, monthly payment, arrangement fee, approval timeline) — fully specified.
- legal_services: includes legal services with costs (legal package cost, survey cost, total legal costs, timeline) — fully specified.
- moving_services: includes moving arrangements with costs (service type, cost, timeline) — fully specified.
- setup_services: includes utility and setup services (package, included services, timeline) — fully specified.
- total_budget: includes complete cost breakdown (property price, down payment, legal/survey, moving, utilities, arrangement fee, total upfront costs, monthly payment, reserves, timeline) — fully specified.
All 6 items are present and contain the necessary details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final property solution includes specific selections that correspond to items in the ground-truth ratings: the property is 'Family House in Toronto' which matches 'Family House' in purchase_properties; financing is 'Traditional Mortgage'; legal services include 'Premium Legal Package' and 'Full Structural Survey'; moving is 'Standard Moving'; and utility setup is 'Premium Utilities'. I mapped each of these to their exact ratings from the ground-truth dataset.
